title: Streaming Multi-Scale Adaptive Kernel Conformal Prediction
description: A novel conformal prediction framework for streaming data that maintains a hierarchy of kernel scales with online adaptive bandwidth selection. Unlike existing multi-model approaches that aggregate diverse predictors, SMAK-CP adapts the kernel geometry of a single model to local data structure, providing efficient and locally adaptive prediction sets with distribution-free coverage guarantees under distribution drift.
motivation: Current kernel-based conformal prediction methods use fixed bandwidths that cannot adapt to varying local data density or distribution drift. Multi-model ensemble approaches like MOCP require evaluating multiple models per prediction. Localized methods like RLCP provide theoretical local coverage guarantees but operate with fixed bandwidths in batch settings. There is no existing method that combines online bandwidth adaptation with multi-scale kernel structure for streaming conformal prediction.
proposed_approach: SMAK-CP maintains K kernel scales with bandwidths following a geometric progression. The base bandwidth adapts online based on local coverage discrepancy feedback using exponential moving average. A hierarchical warm-up protocol addresses cold-start in sparse regions. For each prediction, the method selects appropriate scale(s) based on local effective sample size. Three variants are proposed: SMAK-S (single-scale selection), SMAK-I (multi-scale intersection with calibrated thresholds), and SMAK-W (weighted aggregation). The framework provides marginal coverage guarantees under bounded distribution drift (not assuming exchangeability) with theoretical regret bounds for bandwidth adaptation.
key_innovations: Online bandwidth adaptation for kernel-based conformal prediction via coverage feedback
Multi-scale kernel hierarchy with geometric bandwidth progression
Scale selection based on local effective sample size with fallback mechanisms
Calibrated multi-scale intersection strategy with theoretical coverage guarantees
Hierarchical cold-start initialization for sparse data regions
Streaming adaptation under distribution drift without assuming exchangeability

related_work.relationship: Foundation for RKHS-based conditional conformal prediction. SMAK-CP extends to streaming settings with adaptive bandwidth.

related_work.paper: SpeedCP: Fast Kernel-based Conditional Conformal Prediction
related_work.relationship: Accelerates RKHS-CP computation but uses fixed bandwidth and batch setting. SMAK-CP complements with online adaptation.

related_work.paper: Multi-model Ensemble Conformal Prediction in Dynamic Environments
related_work.relationship: Uses multiple ACI instances with model selection. SMAK-CP differs by using single model with adaptive kernel geometry, avoiding ensemble evaluation cost.

related_work.paper: Conformal Prediction with Local Weights: Randomization Enables Robust Guarantees
related_work.relationship: Provides theoretical local coverage with fixed bandwidth. SMAK-CP extends to streaming with adaptive bandwidth and multi-scale structure.

related_work.paper: Multi-Scale Conformal Prediction: A Theoretical Framework
related_work.relationship: Proposes multi-scale CP via set intersection with fixed scales. SMAK-CP adds online bandwidth adaptation and streaming capability.

related_work.paper: Improved Online Conformal Prediction via Strongly Adaptive Online Learning
related_work.relationship: Provides theoretical foundation for strongly adaptive online learning in conformal prediction. SMAK-CP complements with geometric (multi-scale) adaptation.

related_work.paper: Online Kernel Learning With Adaptive Bandwidth by Optimal Control Approach
related_work.relationship: Addresses adaptive bandwidth in online kernel learning for prediction accuracy. SMAK-CP addresses coverage-valid uncertainty quantification with multi-scale structure under distribution drift.
Provides theoretical framework for conformal prediction under distribution drift via weighted quantiles. SMAK-CP builds on this for kernel-based streaming adaptation.

related_work.paper: Conformal Inference for Online Prediction with Arbitrary Distribution Shifts
related_work.relationship: Establishes coverage guarantees for online conformal prediction under arbitrary distribution shifts. SMAK-CP extends to multi-scale kernel setting.

hypothesis: Online adaptive bandwidth selection within a multi-scale kernel hierarchy provides superior local coverage and prediction set efficiency compared to fixed-bandwidth kernel methods in streaming settings with heterogeneous data density and distribution drift.

confirming: Achieves target marginal coverage (0.9 ± 0.02) across all datasets
Significantly lower MSCE than fixed-bandwidth methods (p < 0.05)
10-30% tighter prediction sets than RLCP in heterogeneous regions
Runtime within 2x of single-scale methods, faster than MOCP
Cold-start strategy reduces coverage violations by >50% in first 200 steps
refuting: Online bandwidth adaptation fails to converge (coverage oscillates)
Multi-scale structure provides no benefit over well-tuned single-scale
Adaptation overhead makes method impractical (> 5x slower)
SMAK-I intersection produces overly conservative sets despite calibration

computational_requirements.feasibility: Experiments involve kernel computations on tabular data (n ~ 10K), quantile regression, and online updates. All operations are CPU-friendly matrix operations. Expected runtime per experiment: 30-60 minutes. Total experiment suite: ~6 hours.
